mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, which boosts corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative mean return over 60 days in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which signals near-term earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of acceleration episodes, or the signal shows zero predictive power during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >70% of high-inflation periods, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of positive payroll growth months, or the correlation between payroll growth and forward returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures where consumption outpaces GDP indicates strong consumer-driven demand expansion, which accelerates earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes insignificant when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, which triggers negative earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of decline episodes, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.
